steps.title: Understand what a margin of error means
steps.content: A survey reports an estimate (41%) with a margin of error (6%). The margin of error does NOT mean the estimate is exact - it defines a range of plausible values for the true population percentage.

steps.title: Compute the confidence interval
steps.content:
Apply the margin of error symmetrically around the estimate:
$$41\% - 6\% = 35\%$$
$$41\% + 6\% = 47\%$$
The plausible range is 35% to 47%.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A - claims exactly 41% support the change. A margin of error means we cannot pin down an exact value; the true percent could be anywhere from 35% to 47%. This is too definitive.
  • B - claims the true percent is greater than 41%. The interval extends both above and below 41%, so values below 41% (e.g., 36%) are equally plausible.
  • C - states plausible values are between 35% and 47%. This is the precise meaning of a 6% margin of error around 41%. That matches Choice C — Plausible values of the percent of the students at the school who support a schedule change are between 35% and 47%..
  • D - gives numbers of students (394-406) rather than percentages. 41% of 400 is 164 students, not near 394-406. Wrong units and wrong values.
